title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes at 11, Bennett supports Joslin Diabetes Center and seeks to inspire others.
Bennett credits Joslin Diabetes Center with providing guidance and confidence to live a full life with diabetes.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter receives her fifth Oscar nomination for 'Sinners', making her the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history.
Carter was the first Black person to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design and has won multiple Academy Awards.
Carter, from Springfield, Massachusetts, emphasizes designing authentic period pieces and is working on a project with Denzel Washington.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if property-tax override is not approved by voters, officials warn.
The district requires $18.5 million over three years to maintain staffing and programs without significant cuts.
Brookline voters will decide on the tax override at the May 5 annual election, as rising costs strain budgets.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The proposed PETS Act in Massachusetts aims to reduce pet sales from puppy mills and expand animal welfare protections.
The bill would ban pet shop sales of dogs and cats, promoting adoptions through partnerships with shelters and rescue groups.
The act also addresses housing and insurance barriers, seeking to allow more pet ownership in public housing and insurance coverage despite pet breed.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts state officials urge water conservation due to persistent drought, worsened despite heavy winter snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts face critical drought levels; Western regions see mild drought conditions.
Residents are advised to fix leaks, save water, and use water-efficient appliances amid ongoing drought concerns.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph explores why some straight women obscure or exclude boyfriends from social media posts.
Joseph's viral essay suggests women are rethinking traditional heterosexuality and male-centered public identities.
The essay sparked widespread debate, music adaptation, and discussions on modern relationship dynamics.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, will run the Boston Marathon for The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp, aiding children with medical conditions.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, Reagan Carey, benefited from the camp during her battle with rare bone cancer; she now serves as a counselor.
O'Neill aims to support families and ensure the camp's continued impact, highlighting the courage of those it serves.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles have decided not to trade wide receiver A.J. Brown at this time despite interest from the Patriots.
Trade discussions may resume around June 1 when salary cap relief allows the Eagles to benefit financially by trading Brown.
The Patriots reportedly offered a first- and third-round pick for Brown, which was rejected by the Eagles.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to play against the Wizards after sitting out for injury management of his right Achilles.
Tatum has been playing on a minutes restriction since his season debut following a right ruptured Achilles injury.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable to play, while Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' featuring Alan Ritchson streams on Netflix; follows a soldier with PTSD facing a war machine.
'Zootopia 2' with Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin streams on Disney+; explores police partners solving a mystery.
Steve Carell stars in 'Rooster' on HBO Max; a comedy about an author starting anew on a college campus.
